summary: This notebook implements a trajectory prediction pipeline for the NFL Big Data Bowl competition, forecasting future player positions over a 94-frame horizon. It unifies play directions to a single orientation, engineers extensive kinematic and interaction features, and builds sliding-window sequences for a GRU-Attention model. Training employs a custom temporal Huber loss with smoothness regularization, GroupKFold validation, and multiple data augmentations. Inference leverages multi-seed and multi-fold averaging combined with Test-Time Augmentation to produce robust displacement predictions.
competition_flow: Loads NFL trajectory CSVs, unifies play directions, computes kinematic and interaction features, builds sliding-window sequences, trains a GRU-Attention model with a custom temporal Huber loss using GroupKFold across multiple seeds, and generates submissions via multi-seed/fold averaging with TTA.
data_reading: Reads weekly train input/output CSVs and test CSVs from the competition directory.
Concatenates data across weeks and casts game_id, play_id, and nfl_id to int64.
data_processing: Mirrors coordinates and angles for 'right' plays to unify direction to 'left'.
Pads sequences to a fixed window size, applies StandardScaler per fold, and inverts coordinates back to original play direction post-inference.
features_engineering: Distance-to-ball metrics and target alignment features.
Multi-window rolling statistics and extended lags.
Field position relative to sidelines/endzones and role-specific flags.
Sinusoidal time features and curvature/landing geometry.
Receiver-defender interaction features (distances, closing rates, leverage, density).
models: SeqModel (GRU + Multihead Attention pooling + ResidualMLP head)
loss_functions: TemporalHuber (Huber loss with exponential time decay + 2nd-order velocity smoothness regularization)
ensembling: Averages predictions across 5 random seeds and 5 cross-validation folds, applies Test-Time Augmentation (6 iterations with Gaussian noise and temporal context flipping) per model, and computes the final mean displacement before adding to the last known position.
insights: Unifying play direction to a single orientation simplifies sequence modeling and avoids directional bias.
Using a custom temporal Huber loss with smoothness regularization improves trajectory continuity.
Multi-seed and multi-fold averaging combined with TTA provides robustness against overfitting and data quirks.
critical_findings: Ball landing features must be disabled if unavailable in the test set to prevent errors.
Aggressive interaction features are commented out for a conservative version to avoid unstable behavior.
